description: Eval set for the F1 RAG pipeline. Each question has an expected answer (verified via web search against Wikipedia/F1.com on 2026-05-19), a category, and notes. Use this to measure retrieval + generation quality before and after each Stage 2 change.
corpus_scope: Wikipedia pages for the 24 races of the 2024 F1 season

questions.question: Who won the 2024 Monaco Grand Prix?
questions.notes: His first home win and first win since 2022 Austrian GP.

questions.question: Where was the 2024 Las Vegas Grand Prix held?
questions.expected_answer: Las Vegas Strip Circuit in Paradise, Nevada (also widely referred to as Las Vegas)
questions.notes: Either 'Las Vegas Strip Circuit' or 'Paradise, Nevada' should be acceptable.

questions.question: Who took pole position for the 2024 British Grand Prix?
questions.notes: Mercedes 1-2 in qualifying with Hamilton second; Norris third.

questions.question: Which team did Lando Norris drive for in 2024?
On what date was the 2024 Abu Dhabi Grand Prix held?
questions.notes: Final race of the 2024 season at Yas Marina Circuit.

questions.question: Who finished on the podium at the 2024 São Paulo Grand Prix?
questions.expected_answer: Max Verstappen (1st), Esteban Ocon (2nd), Pierre Gasly (3rd)
questions.notes: Verstappen won from P17 in wet conditions; Alpine double podium.

questions.question: What were the weather conditions at the 2024 São Paulo Grand Prix?
questions.expected_answer: Wet/rainy. Qualifying was postponed from Saturday to Sunday morning due to rain; the race was held in wet conditions with all cars on intermediate or full wet tyres.
questions.notes: Tests whether retrieval surfaces weather-related chunks.

questions.question: What happened to Sergio Pérez at the 2024 Monaco Grand Prix?
questions.expected_answer: He crashed on lap 1 in a collision with both Haas drivers (Magnussen and Hülkenberg), causing a red flag.
questions.notes: Tests whether retrieval finds incident-specific chunks within a race report.

questions.question: Who was declared the winner of the 2024 Belgian Grand Prix after the disqualification?
questions.expected_answer: Lewis Hamilton. George Russell finished first on the road but was disqualified for an underweight car; Hamilton was promoted to victory.
questions.notes: Tests whether the system handles the DSQ correctly rather than just naming Russell.

questions.question: How many wins did Lando Norris get in the 2024 season?
questions.expected_answer: 4 wins — Miami, Dutch (Netherlands), Singapore, and Abu Dhabi Grands Prix.
questions.notes: Requires aggregating across multiple race pages. Likely to fail in Stage 1; this is the kind of question that motivates the structured-data path in Stage 3.

questions.question: At which races did Charles Leclerc win in 2024?
questions.expected_answer: 3 wins — Monaco, Italian (Monza), and United States (Austin) Grands Prix.
questions.notes: Same aggregation problem as Norris wins.

questions.question: Compare Max Verstappen and Lando Norris's performances in the 2024 season.
questions.expected_answer: Verstappen won the Drivers' Championship for the fourth consecutive year (clinched at Las Vegas) with several wins; Norris finished second overall with 4 wins and was Verstappen's main title challenger but never overtook him in the standings.
questions.notes: Broad comparison — naive RAG will struggle. Tests whether retrieval surfaces season-context chunks vs single-race chunks.

questions.question: Who won the 2023 Monaco Grand Prix?
questions.expected_answer: REFUSAL — the system should say it does not have enough information, since 2023 is outside our 2024-only corpus.
questions.notes: Critical test: a confident wrong answer here means the LLM is using its training data, not the retrieved context.

questions.question: What is Lando Norris's helmet design?
questions.expected_answer: REFUSAL — race reports do not typically describe driver helmet designs in detail. The system should acknowledge it doesn't have this info.
questions.notes: Tests grounding: model should not hallucinate from general training knowledge.

questions.question: Who is Max Verstappen's race engineer?
questions.expected_answer: Gianpiero Lambiase (nickname 'GP' / 'Lambo'). Acceptable if retrieved; refusal also acceptable if the corpus doesn't contain it.
questions.notes: Genuinely uncertain whether race-report Wikipedia pages mention this. Score either correct answer or refusal as acceptable.

questions.question: What was the most exciting race of the 2024 season?
questions.expected_answer: Subjective question. A good answer should acknowledge the subjectivity and point to candidates that the corpus describes as notable — e.g., São Paulo (Verstappen from P17 in the wet) or Brazilian GP chaos.
questions.notes: Tests how the system handles vague questions. Shouldn't confidently name one race as objectively 'best'.

questions.question: Tell me about the 2024 championship battle.
questions.expected_answer: Broad summary. Should mention Verstappen leading early, McLaren and Norris closing the gap mid-season, and Verstappen ultimately clinching the title at Las Vegas. Constructors went to McLaren at Abu Dhabi.
questions.notes: Cross-document aggregation. Stage 1 will likely give a single-race answer instead of a season summary.
